title: No Repeat Child Sex Offenders Act
short_title: No Repeat Child Sex Offenders Act
plain_summary: This bill makes the punishments much more severe for people who commit sex crimes against children. Instead of the current prison sentences that can range from two years to thirty years, anyone convicted of these crimes would either get life in prison or the death penalty. The law covers many different types of crimes including sex trafficking of children, moving children across state lines for illegal purposes, sexually abusing children, selling children, and helping to arrange travel for illegal sexual acts. The goal is to stop repeat offenders by giving the harshest possible punishments for these serious crimes against kids.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Repeat Child Sex Offenders Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Sex Trafficking
sections.text: This section changes the punishments for sex trafficking crimes. For transporting people for illegal sex acts, the punishment will now be a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of up to ten years in prison. For promoting or helping with sex trafficking, the punishment will be a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of up to ten or twenty-five years in prison depending on the specific crime. For forcing or tricking people into illegal sex acts, the punishment will be a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of up to twenty years in prison or the current life sentence options. For sex trafficking of children specifically, all cases will now be punished with a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of the current range of ten to twenty-five years or life in prison.

sections.title: Section 3 Transportation of Minors
sections.text: This section changes punishments for crimes involving moving children across state or country borders for illegal sexual purposes. All these crimes will now be punished with a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of the current punishments which range from ten to thirty years in prison. For people who commit these crimes more than once, the punishment will also be a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of the current rule that makes the prison sentence three times longer than normal.

sections.title: Section 4 Exploitation of Children
sections.text: This section creates a new punishment for people who arrange, convince, get, or help with travel when they know the person is traveling to commit illegal sexual acts for money or business gain. These people will be fined and punished with either the death penalty or life in prison.

sections.title: Section 5 Selling Children
sections.text: This section changes the punishment for selling children. Instead of at least thirty years in prison or life in prison, the punishment will now be either the death penalty or life in prison.

sections.title: Section 6 Sexual Abuse
sections.text: This section changes punishments for different types of sexual abuse crimes. For the most serious sexual abuse crimes, the punishment will be a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of at least thirty years or life in prison. For sexual abuse of minors (children under eighteen), all cases will be punished with a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of up to fifteen years in prison. For abusive sexual contact crimes, all cases will be punished with a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison instead of the current punishments which range from two years in prison to life in prison. For sexual abuse crimes that result in death, the punishment will be a fine and either the death penalty or life in prison.
